flows.title: User Flow 1 — Sign-up & OTP verification
flows.desc: Done when a new user can sign up end to end through the new OTP screen with no Get-Started loop (ID-20372), resend and expired codes behave, and login error states show clear messages. Fresh state; ~15 min.

items.title: Login: User can sign up to their account with new credentials using email and password option
Login: Kill app on OTP verification screen, then relaunch
Login: Verification code resend button
Login: Verification code resend button - try and use old code
Login: User cannot sign up to a new account with existing credentials
Login: Login with wrong password for an existing account
Login: Login with empty fields
Login: Log in with Existing credentials

flows.title: User Flow 2 — Onboarding profile & relationship context
flows.desc: Done when a new user completes the avatar/identity step (name, gender, fighter identity), answers the relationship check-in questions, the answers survive a force-quit (Profile API, ID-20106/20112/20113), and back-navigation works. ~15 min, continues from Flow 1's fresh account.

items.title: Onboarding: Complete avatar & identity step (name, gender, fighter identity)
Onboarding: Name field validation (empty, very long, emoji)
Onboarding: Back navigation through onboarding steps
Onboarding: Relationship context questions appear (duration, type, goals)
Onboarding: Multi-select questions allow selecting and deselecting several options
Onboarding: Relationship answers persist after force-quit
Onboarding: Profile saves to backend (persists after relaunch)
Onboarding: Screens match Figma (design referee)

flows.title: User Flow 3 — Shadow Boxing practice
flows.desc: Done when a solo user can pick a persona from the library, hold a coached practice conversation with AI whispers that are visibly private, and save, revisit and delete the transcript (ID-20114/20115); an exit control stays reachable throughout. ~20 min.

items.title: Shadow Boxing: Entry point launches a practice round
Shadow Boxing: Persona library lists personas and one can be selected
Shadow Boxing: AI opponent responds in conversation turns
Shadow Boxing: Coaching whispers appear during user's turn and are visually distinct
Shadow Boxing: Emergency exit / leave is always reachable during practice
Shadow Boxing: Kill app mid-practice, relaunch
Shadow Boxing: Transcript saved when practice ends
Shadow Boxing: Revisit a saved transcript
Shadow Boxing: Delete a transcript (with confirmation)

flows.title: User Flow 4 — Partner invite
flows.desc: Done when every invite entry point works: share buttons visible on screen (ID-20385), the link opens the app without Page Not Found (ID-20381), invalid codes get a clear error, and the QR scanner loads without hanging (ID-20383/20384). ~10 min.

items.title: Invite: Share buttons fully visible on screen
Invite: Invite Your Partner button + Send invite with link
Invite: Opening an invite link does not crash (no Page Not Found)
Invite: Enter an invalid invite code
Invite: Invite Your Partner button + Scan code (QR)

flows.title: User Flow 5 — Couple Stats
flows.desc: Done when Couple Stats opens with the corrected logo and "Spars" header (ID-20399/20400) and its empty state respects the no-out-scoring invariant. ~5 min.

items.title: Stats: Couple Stats shows correct logo and "Spars" header
Stats: Couple Stats empty state and no partner-vs-partner scoring

__m.purpose: Box and Ring — build 0.0.1 (9): verify Onboarding Flow 1 (avatar/identity, relationship context, Shadow Boxing) and regress the five fix areas (OTP sign-up loop, QR scanner, invite links, share-button visibility, Couple Stats naming). iPhone 15 Pro / iOS 18.5. Narrate the item number before each test.

__meta.recordings: 3 suggested (sign-up + onboarding / shadow boxing / invite + stats)

f.steps:
Precondition: N/A (fresh state — log out or reinstall).
Test data: NEW email + Password: Passw0rd!
1. Get Started → sign up with the new email and password
2. Submit
f.expected: User creates Account by entering their email and password, user get's verification message in email, uses it to finish log in set up
f.notes2: RETEST of the old sign-up loop (ID-20372) — narrate if you get bounced back to Get Started at any point

f.steps:
Precondition: fresh sign-up in progress with a NEW email.
1. Sign up with a new email + password
2. Reach the OTP verification screen
3. Force-quit the app
4. Relaunch the app
f.expected: App resumes to verification (or login) without looping back to "Get Started"; entering the emailed code still completes sign-up.

f.steps:
1. User waits 30 seconds until a resend is available.
2. User clicks on "Didn't reveice a code? Resend"
3. User enters new code into account verification slot
f.expected: User receives a new verification code to their email. This code can be entered into the app resulting in log in

f.steps:
Precondition: User must have received a verification code previously.
1. User waits 30 seconds until a resend is available.
2. User clicks on "Didn't reveice a code? Resend"
3. User enters old code into account verification slot
f.expected: User receives a new verification code to their email. The user entering the old code into verification received a notification "invalid code"

f.steps:
Precondition: System already has user in system with same credentials (Email: phill@mightybyte.us / Password: Passw0rd!).
1. Create a new account with email and password
2. Exit out of App
f.expected: User trying to create new account with existing credentials see's message that an account already exists and they should  log in rather than create a new account.
f.notes2: Known bug from last session (status Bug) — narrate exact behaviour; don't re-file unless it changed

f.steps:
Precondition: an existing verified account.
1. Go to the login screen
2. Enter the existing email with a WRONG password
3. Submit
f.expected: Login rejected with a clear, friendly error message; no crash, no vague wording. Screenshot the exact message for the copy check.

f.steps:
1. Go to the login screen
2. Leave email and password empty
3. Tap log in
f.expected: Login blocked with clear field-level prompts; button disabled or a clear message; no crash.

f.steps:
Precondition: existing verified credentials (Email: phill@mightybyte.us / Password: Passw0rd!).
1. Log in to app with existing credentials
f.expected: User is logged into app and taken to home screen / dashboard

f.steps:
Precondition: new account mid-onboarding.
1. Sign up with a new account
2. At the avatar/identity step enter a name, select gender, choose fighter identity
3. Continue
f.expected: Selections accepted; next onboarding step loads; no error.

f.steps:
Test data: "" · 60-char string · "😀🔥"
1. At the name field try to continue with it empty
2. Enter a 60-character name and continue
3. Enter emoji-only and continue
f.expected: Empty is blocked with a clear message; long name is either cleanly limited or displayed without breaking layout; emoji does not crash or corrupt the display.

f.steps:
1. At the identity step tap back
2. At a relationship question tap back
f.expected: Back returns to the previous step with earlier answers retained; no dead end.
f.notes2: Back was broken in the last build (bug filed) — narrate current behaviour

f.steps:
1. Continue onboarding past the identity step
2. Observe the relationship check-in step
f.expected: Relationship questions are presented (duration, type, goal at minimum) in the AI-coach chat UI; user can answer and progress.

f.steps:
1. On a select-all-that-apply question (e.g. goals or triggers) select 3 options
2. Deselect 1
3. Continue
f.expected: Multiple selections allowed; deselect works; the saved state reflects the final choices.

f.steps:
1. Answer 2 relationship questions
2. Force-quit the app
3. Relaunch
f.expected: Onboarding resumes at or after the answered questions; answers are not lost.

f.steps:
Precondition: identity step completed.
1. Complete the identity step
2. Force-quit the app
3. Relaunch and log in
f.expected: Name, gender and fighter identity are retained (not re-asked; visible in profile if a profile view exists).
f.notes2: Backend save is unverifiable from the phone — persistence after relaunch is the proxy; narrate what's retained

f.steps:
1. Compare the identity + relationship screens against the Figma onboarding frames
2. Check layout, typography, colors, button states
f.expected: No obvious drift from the design; any mismatch noted with a screenshot.
f.notes2: Figma walkthrough was skipped this session — open the Figma file side-by-side and compare manually

f.steps:
Precondition: logged in; no partner connection required.
1. From the dashboard (or waiting-for-partner state) open Shadow Boxing / practice
f.expected: Practice mode opens without a partner connected.

f.steps:
1. Open the persona library
2. Browse the personas
3. Select one (e.g. husband/wife persona)
f.expected: Library shows multiple personas with descriptions; selection is confirmed and used for the round.

f.steps:
1. Start a practice round
2. Send 2-3 messages
f.expected: The persona replies in character within a reasonable time; the turn structure holds.

f.steps: 1. During your turns, watch for AI coaching whispers
f.expected: Whisper/coaching messages appear and are styled clearly differently from the persona conversation (private-channel styling).

f.steps:
1. During a round, look for an exit/leave/timeout affordance
2. Use it
f.expected: An exit control is visible throughout the practice round and works.
f.notes2: Invariant — if there is NO visible exit during practice, that's a finding even though it's solo mode

f.steps:
1. Mid-round, force-quit the app
2. Relaunch
f.expected: Graceful resume or clean restart; no corrupt or blank state; the transcript is not half-broken.

f.steps:
1. End the practice round
2. Look for the saved transcript
f.expected: A transcript of the session is saved and listed.

f.steps:
Precondition: at least one saved transcript.
1. Open the saved transcript
f.expected: Full conversation is readable; narrate whether whispers are included or marked private.

f.steps:
Precondition: at least one saved transcript.
1. Delete the transcript
2. Observe the confirmation dialog
3. Check the list after deleting
f.expected: A confirmation is asked; the transcript is gone after delete. Edge: after deleting the last transcript the empty state renders correctly.

f.steps:
1. Open Invite Your Partner
2. Observe the share options without scrolling
f.expected: Share buttons are on screen (previously hidden below the viewport) and tappable.

f.steps:
1. Click Invite your partner link
2. Select "Send Invite"
3. Select "Share link"
4. Copy the generated link
f.expected: A shareable invite link is generated and can be copied/shared via the native share sheet.

f.steps:
Precondition: an invite link generated from the account.
1. Copy the invite link
2. Open it on the device (paste in Safari/Notes and tap) with the app installed, cold start
3. Repeat with the app already running (warm)
f.expected: The app opens to a sensible screen; no "Page Not Found" crash. Full pairing needs a second account; narrate what is visible.
f.notes2: Full receiver-side pairing is unverifiable with one account — narrate what the app shows

f.steps:
1. Open the invite / enter-code screen
2. Enter an invalid code (e.g. 000000)
3. Submit
f.expected: A clear error message appears promptly; no infinite loading.

f.steps:
1. Open Invite Your Partner → Scan code
2. Observe the scanner view
Edge: deny camera permission first, then re-grant via Settings and retry.
f.expected: Camera/scanner loads without getting stuck loading (ID-20383/20384); permission denial shows a graceful message, not a hang.
f.notes2: Needs a real camera — if the device/BrowserStack blocks it, mark Blocked, not Fail

f.steps: 1. Open Couple Stats from the dashboard
f.expected: Section header reads "Spars" (not "Bouts"); the logo asset renders correctly (not stretched or placeholder).
f.notes2: Screenshot the header for the copy check

f.steps: 1. With no completed spars, open Couple Stats
f.expected: A sensible empty state; nothing on any shared view frames one partner as beating the other.
